channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Nearly 1,000 workers laid off at SK Battery plant in Georgia as companies cancel EVs and Trump Admin eliminates auto company incentives
item.description: Ford announced it was cancelling the all-electric Ford F-150 Lightning in December. SK Battery America Inc., a supplier of the Ford truck, laid off ne...

item.title: Silicon Valley investor Vinod Khosla predicts education will be free, and the future of college ‘is a real question’
item.description: The billionaire VC says AI raises questions about how we value expertise.

item.title: February was the biggest month in venture history, thanks only to OpenAI, Anthropic, and Waymo
item.description: Startup funding surged to a historic $189 billion in February, driven almost entirely by three AI mega-rounds that captured 83% of the total.

item.title: OpenAI investor Vinod Khosla believes AI will be able to do 80% of all jobs by 2030. Here’s how life could be affordable after mass unemployment
item.description: The Silicon Valley billionaire laid out a vision to Fortune of a deflationary economy—but also free healthcare and education, along with a tax overhau...

item.title: A Minneapolis Fed report details how much Trump’s immigration crackdown hurt businesses and workers. ‘There are not any people to hire’
item.description: "We are hiring now to replace these workers or get more reliable alternatives, but there are not any people to hire."

item.title: Jesse Jackson Chicago Funeral: Obama, Biden, Clinton to Attend
item.description: The large-scale, public event in Chicago on Friday caps two weeks of memorials for Mr. Jackson, the civil rights leader, who died at 84.

item.title: Democrats Blame Trump For Jobs Report as Republicans Play Defense
item.description: The weaker-than-expected numbers quickly became a source of tension between the two parties ahead of the midterm elections.

item.title: How The Times Tracks Down the Connections Behind Trump’s Pardons
item.description: Reporters tapped sources, combed through public records and scrutinized social media to penetrate the web of influence and money underlying the presid...

item.title: New England Patriots’ Plane Ferried Americans Back From Middle East
item.description: The flight was arranged and paid for by the State Department, officials said, as part of the evacuations of citizens stranded by attacks in the Middle...

item.title: Live Updates: Oil and Gas Prices Jump as Iran War’s Economic Cost Climbs
item.description: The price of oil surged to more than $90 a barrel and U.S. gasoline prices rose again. Israeli airstrikes pummeled Tehran and Lebanon, and President T...

item.title: Justice Dept. Pushes for Charges Against Cuban Leaders
item.description: The move comes as President Trump is ratcheting up his rhetorical assault on Cuba’s leadership.

item.title: What to know about the jobs report.
item.description: Employers shed 92,000 jobs in February and the unemployment rate rose to 4.4 percent.

item.title: The A.I. Labor Crisis Is Coming. This Is the Solution.
item.description: America’s strength has always been its private sector. Empowering it can save us from A.I.’s worst effects on jobs.

item.title: Macron speaks with Lebanon’s leader, as France seeks to broker a truce between Israel and Hezbollah.
Israel says it struck an underground bunker at Khamenei’s compound.
